prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, ye be askin' why the vast ceiling o' the world be donned in blue, ye curious landlubber? I'll tell ye what me own two seafarin' eyes have witnessed!

'Tis the grand trickery o' the Almighty's realm! When the sun's golden doubloons o' light sail through the ocean o' air above our heads, the wee bits o' air—smaller than the smallest grain o' sand on Tortuga's shore—they be catchin' and tossin' about the blue light more than the rest!

Aye, the blue light be scattered about like a chest o' treasure flung wide on the deck, while the red and yellow lights sail straight through like a Man o' War with a fair wind! That blue light bounces from all quarters o' the heavens into yer gaze, paintin' the whole sky the color o' the deepest Caribbean waters.

Many a long voyage has given me time to ponder such mysteries while watchin' the endless blue above match the endless blue below. 'Tis a fine thing to understand the ways o' the world when ye spend yer days between sky and sea!

Now pass the rum, for such knowledge be thirsty work!
